steps.title: Identify the dual requirement
steps.content: The prompt says the student wants to provide both an explanation of what Sonic Meditations is and a concrete example of one meditation. The correct choice must accomplish both goals.

steps.title: Test each choice for explanation and example
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Sonic Meditations is not music but rather a series of sound-based meditations that consist of instructions; Meditation XVIII, for instance, instructs participants to "listen to a sound until you no longer recognize it."’: opens with an explanation of what the work is, then pairs it with a specific example from Meditation XVIII. Both requirements met. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘In 1971, Oliveros created Sonic Meditations, a series of meditations that consist of instructions for participants to make, imagine, listen to, or remember sounds.’: describes what the work is but gives no specific example of any single meditation. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘"Walk so silently that the bottoms of your feet become ears" is one example of the instructions found in Oliveros's Sonic Meditations.’: provides an example from Meditation V but never explains what Sonic Meditations is. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘While both meditations consist of instructions, Meditation XVIII instructs participants to "listen," whereas Meditation V instructs participants to "walk."’: compares two meditations but does not explain what Sonic Meditations is as a concept. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
